title: Rethinking Furry Fandom from Associology
description: A research project supported by The Nippon Foundation HUMAI Program. It applies the framework of Associology to analyze and rethink the social structures, relationships, and dynamics within the furry fandom.

title: Establishing Associology as a transdisciplinary field: symmetrical description of academic friction through the case study of BDSM practices
description: Published in the HUMAI Anthology. This study introduces 'Associology' to bridge theoretical sociology and real-world practice. By analyzing texts from BDSM communities, queer studies, and legal cases, it shows how different fields simplify complex physical realities.

title: The ontological politics of the absence of same-sex marriage
description: Published in The Journal of International Public Policy. This paper examines why same-sex marriage remains unlegalized in Japan. Using Actor-Network Theory, it maps how the judiciary, legislature, and administration actively interact to maintain this legal absence despite growing public support.

title: Beyond same-sex marriage: Scope and limits of the Japanese Constitution Article 24
description: Presented at the University of Tsukuba. This research re-evaluates Japan's constitutional view of marriage. By exploring non-traditional relationships like polyamory and fictosexuality, it argues that current legal interpretations are limited more by a human-centric bias than by gender binaries.

title: Reassembling the "Sociology of Associations" as an interdisciplinary episteme
description: A research initiative backed by The Nippon Foundation HUMAI Program. It focuses on reconstructing the 'Sociology of Associations' into an interdisciplinary framework capable of addressing complex, modern social issues.

title: Thinking peace and human rights in the EXPO: How can peace be realised and human rights be guaranteed? Agenda 2025 co-created programme
description: An EXPO 2025 co-created program discussing future social systems for 2050. Diverse participants gather to explore practical definitions of peace and human rights, addressing ongoing global issues and brainstorming actionable steps to realize these values.

title: Report of multifaceted investigation for future society design "Shape New World Project"
description: This report explores a vision for a 2050 society centered on peace and human rights. It examines strategies to eradicate direct and structural violence and foster diversity to prevent social division, drawing on a large-scale public survey and the ethical implementation of emerging technologies.

title: Reassembling the teachers' professional identity: A Sociology of Associations Perspective
description: This study explores how early-career teachers in Ireland build their professional identities. Using Actor-Network Theory, it highlights how new teachers navigate the 'reality shock' between educational ideals and everyday classroom ecosystems, emphasizing the impact of digital tools and AI.

title: Critical analysis of diversity in Japan: From the perspective of Queer Studies
description: Presented at the TSUKUBA CONFERENCE 2023. This study analyzes diversity in Japan through Queer and Peace Studies. It clarifies the distinction between sexual and romantic orientations, challenges unconscious biases surrounding sexuality, and re-examines the historical framing of non-traditional identities.
